title: Man arrested for 'threatening' to kill Kerala CM Satheesan, family
content: A man identified as Sony Thomas was arrested in Thiruvananthapuram for allegedly making threatening phone calls to kill Kerala Chief Minister V D Satheesan and his family. The accused reportedly made two calls to the police command center, stating he disliked the Chief Minister. Police apprehended Thomas from Attingal, suspecting he was under the influence of alcohol during the calls.

title: IND vs AFG: Virat, Hardik injuries open doors for Ishan Kishan and Nitish Reddy
content: Virat Kohli and Hardik Pandya's injuries have forced India into transition mode as they begin preparations for the 2027 ODI World Cup. Ishan Kishan appears set to get the No. 3 role, Nitish Kumar Reddy could replace Hardik, while young pacers Prince and Gurnoor await opportunities.

title: 'Won't let Indus waters reach patrons of terror': Rajnath's strong message to Pakistan
content: Defence minister Rajnath Singh declared India will not allow Indus river waters to reach "patrons of terror," reinforcing a firm stance against Pakistan. He highlighted Operation Sindoor as proof of India's resolve and criticized opposition parties. Singh also detailed India's global role, defence sector growth, and the NDA government's achievements, contrasting them with past corruption.

title: Microsoft CEO’s message for employees: Not every problem needs AI
content: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is guiding employees towards smarter AI use. He emphasizes selecting the right AI model for each task, not just the most advanced. This approach balances performance and costs. Nadella shared his personal use of AI in software development. Microsoft continues to integrate AI across its business and product strategies.

title: ED breaks locks to enter Abhishek Banerjee's residence during overnight raid
content: A joint police team, including central forces, conducted an overnight search at Trinamool leader Abhishek Banerjee's Kolkata residence. The operation, linked to a Paschim Medinipur case, involved breaking locks after no response from occupants. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee rushed to the scene amidst the ensuing political controversy.

title: FIFA World Cup: Pulisic overtakes Ronaldo in assists as USA rout Paraguay
content: Christian Pulisic became the United States' all-time World Cup assist leader, surpassing Cristiano Ronaldo with his third career assist during a dominant 4-1 victory over Paraguay.  Despite a precautionary halftime substitution due to a calf issue, Pulisic's early contributions set the tone for a historic performance by the USMNT on home soil.

title: Watch: US 'executes' Venezuela's 'bloodthirsty' prison gang leader Niño Guerrero in strike
content: US President Donald Trump announced a US military strike, conducted at his direction, killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, known as Niño Guerrero, leader of the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua. Trump stated the operation was coordinated with Venezuelan authorities, linking it to his anti-crime and immigration agenda.  The gang is designated a foreign terrorist organization by Washington.

title: Google donates $50mn to train 300K workers, CEO says infrastructure is key
content: Google is investing $50 million through Google.org to train 300,000 American workers for skilled trade jobs like electricians, welders, and plumbers. This initiative aims to bolster the nation's digital economy by supporting the physical infrastructure built and maintained by these essential workers. The funding will empower various labor unions and trade associations across more than 20 states.

title: 1,569 days and counting: Russia-Ukraine war now longer than World War I
content: The Russia-Ukraine war has surpassed World War I in duration, now exceeding 1,569 days. What began as a swift Russian operation has become Europe's longest and bloodiest conflict since WWII, defying early predictions. Despite diplomatic efforts and Western support for Ukraine, a durable peace remains elusive, with no clear end in sight.

title: 'Driven to London by masked men': Indian-origin man sentenced to 34 years for kidnap, rape
content: An Indian-origin man, Gagandeep Singh, has been sentenced to 34 years in prison in the UK for kidnapping, torturing, and raping a woman. The 34-year-old was convicted of multiple serious offenses after a trial. He will serve 28 years in custody, followed by an extended license period, and will be deported after completing his sentence.

title: 'I am greater than death': How Jaspal Rana gave life to Tagore's 'Mrityunjay'
content: More than a decorated shooter and coach, Jaspal Rana's enduring legacy lay in his generosity and unwavering commitment to nurturing Indian shooting. Through anecdotes from those closest to him and the grief his passing left behind, this piece explores how Rana's life came to embody the spirit of Rabindranath Tagore's Mrityunjay, becoming, in essence, "greater than death".

title: Home to Bezos & Zuckerberg, Florida battles 4-foot-long invasive lizards
content: Wildlife officials in several US states are intensifying efforts to curb the spread of the Argentine black-and-white tegu, an invasive lizard. With the species' main hatching season in June and July, concerns are rising about its impact on native wildlife, farms, and ecosystems as populations expand beyond Florida.

title: Pune techie suicide: 3 booked, including 2 women colleagues from IT firm
content: Authorities in Bhosari are probing a troubling case involving two female IT employees and a social media contact, who are accused of contributing to the death of a 48-year-old tech professional. The investigation kicked off following a suicide note and a complaint from the victim's son highlighting the alleged harassment they faced.

title: Municipality recruitment scam: ED raids 7 premises linked to TMC MLA Madan Mitra
content: The Enforcement Directorate searched seven locations tied to TMC MLA Madan Mitra as part of a municipal recruitment scam probe. Officials allege Mitra received cash and gold bribes via middlemen to secure jobs for unqualified individuals. Investigations suggest over 125 illegal appointments are linked to the former minister, with the ED continuing its examination.

title: Folarin Balogun: The first American to score a World Cup brace since 1930
content: Folarin Balogun announced his commitment to the United States with a stunning World Cup debut, scoring twice against Paraguay. His brace, alongside goal from Reyna, secured a historic 4-1 victory for the US in their opening match.  Balogun's impressive performance marks a significant moment for American football.

title: Ronaldinho backs Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026
content: Brazil and Portugal enter the 2026 FIFA World Cup from very different positions, yet a single comment from Ronaldinho has linked the two football powers. The Brazilian legend made it clear that Brazil remains his first choice, but if the Seleção fall short, he would like to see Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal finally lift football's biggest prize.

title: Why is Neymar out of Brazil vs Morocco? Ancelotti reveals injury update
content: Neymar will miss FIFA World Cup 2026 opener between Brazil and Morocco due to a calf injury suffered in May. Brazil coach Carlo Ancelotti confirmed the forward is recovering well and could return next week. Neymar has not played for Brazil since 2023. Brazil begin their Group C campaign under pressure as they chase their first World Cup title since 2002.

title: Floyd Mayweather Jr faces setback as Showtime responds in $340M lawsuit
content: Floyd Mayweather Jr is preparing for his return to the boxing ring, but a major legal challenge has emerged ahead of his comeback. The undefeated boxing legend filed a $340 million lawsuit against Showtime earlier this year, accusing the network and former executive Stephen Espinoza of mishandling money linked to some of his biggest fights. However, the case has now taken a new turn after Showtime responded with a counterclaim in court.

title: Pawan Kalyan's wife recalls years of humiliation; says his victory felt human
content: Pawan Kalyan's wife, Anna Lezhneva, shared the emotional journey behind his political rise as Andhra Pradesh's Deputy Chief Minister. She revealed years of exhaustion, humiliation, and public criticism he endured, alongside her own struggles at home. Lezhneva expressed immense pride in his perseverance, calling his victory a human triumph after immense pain.

title: From Lebanon ceasefire to nukes: What's in Iran's 'Islamabad Agreement'?
content: Iran has outlined conditions for a potential US agreement, prioritizing sanctions relief and maritime access over immediate nuclear program discussions. Tehran insists on lifting the US naval blockade and a new arrangement for the Strait of Hormuz, while also seeking to include Lebanon in the deal.

title: Siddaramaiah denies favouritism charge over Rs 72-crore grants for OBC organisations
content: Former CM Siddaramaiah defended a Rs 71.8 crore preliminary approval for community halls and hostels, dismissing bias claims. He explained that the higher allocation to Kuruba organizations stemmed from more applications, not favoritism. Funds are allocated based on a continuous reservation formula, with final release contingent on stringent documentation and progress reviews.

title: Cristiano Ronaldo fired back at critics as World Cup doubts continue growing
content: Cristiano Ronaldo pushed back against fitness concerns ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup after ESPN FC shared his response to reporters. The Portugal captain said, “Physically? I’m fine. Have you not seen my matches?” While some fans supported his confidence, others questioned his recent performances, keeping the debate around his World Cup future firmly in the spotlight.

title: The Taylor Swift effect is real and Christen Goff has the DMs to prove it
content: Christen Goff, wife of Detroit Lions quarterback Jared Goff, appeared on PEOPLE's WAG World series and credited Taylor Swift with fundamentally reshaping her social media audience. The Sports Illustrated Swimsuit model said her followers were once dominated by male fantasy football users but have since shifted to include large numbers of women interested in NFL life. Christen also spoke about her first season as a mother, bringing newborn daughter Romy to games just three months after her birth, and her excitement about the upcoming season with a daughter old enough to engage with game day.

title: HC tells Uttarakhand govt not to deport Pak Sikhs if they pose no threat
content: The Uttarakhand High Court has halted the deportation of a Pakistani Sikh family residing in Dehradun on a long-term visa. The court directed the state government not to deport them unless they pose a national security risk. The family, in India since 2019, challenged a 24-hour departure notice, citing their valid visa and children's education.

title: IND vs AFG ODIs: Spotlight on Rohit Sharma as World Cup auditions begin for 2027
content: Rohit Sharma faces an audition for his ODI future, needing to prove his fitness and form ahead of the next World Cup. Captain Shubman Gill grapples with selection decisions, potentially looking beyond the veteran. The series against Afghanistan also serves as a platform to test new batting combinations with Virat Kohli sidelined.

title: 'This is my Super Bowl': Derrick Lewis eyes one more defining moment at UFC Freedom 250
content: Years after becoming the UFC's knockout king, Derrick Lewis finds himself with something every fighter chases - one more unforgettable stage. The heavyweight veteran, who owns a record 16 UFC knockouts, heads to UFC Freedom 250 against Josh Hokit with the White House serving as an extraordinary backdrop. Calling it his "Super Bowl" and "championship fight", Lewis hopes what people remember is "probably the best performance" of his career.

title: Record SpaceX IPO rockets Trill'Elon'aire Musk as stock ends at $161
content: Despite the excitement, SpaceX remains unprofitable and generates significantly less revenue than several technology companies with comparable market valuations. The listing proceeded without major disruptions, in contrast to some high-profile public offerings of the past. Trading began late on Friday morning and the stock eventually closed at $160.95 per share, giving the company a market capitalisation of approximately $2.1 trillion.

title: IND vs AFG: Orange alert, thunderstorms and hail threat loom over 1st ODI
content: Rain and thunderstorms threaten to disrupt the first ODI between India and Afghanistan in Dharamsala. With key players like Virat Kohli and Hardik Pandya already out, the weather forecast predicts wet conditions and a significant chance of precipitation on match day. Fans are concerned that the DLS method might be needed if play is affected.

title: One year after Air India Ahmedabad crash, no report released
content: The London-bound AI171 crashed immediately after taking off from Ahmedabad airport on June 12, 2025. Of the 242 people on board, 241 were killed, and 19 other victims were from the ground at the hostel complex of Ahmedabad’s B J Medical College that the plane crashed into. The closest international parallel to AI171 may be Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302, the 2019 Boeing 737 MAX crash that killed 157 people.

title: Katy Perry kisses Justin Trudeau after headlining World Cup opening ceremony- WATCH
content: Katy Perry and Justin Trudeau made a public display of affection at the FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony in Los Angeles. Perry delivered a powerful performance, later seen rushing to Trudeau for a kiss. The couple also held hands while watching a match, following their red carpet debut at the Tribeca Film Festival.

title: 'No writs after polls begin': Supreme Court rejects Meenakshi Natarajan's plea
content: After an hour-long proceeding, during which senior advocate Mukul Rohatgi for the BJP candidate whose election was facilitated by Natarajan’s disqualification and solicitor general Tushar Mehta for MP govt cited a judgment where SC had refused to entertain a similar plea against rejection of nomination papers, the bench said the SC or HC cannot entertain writ petitions in exercise of their jurisdictions under Articles 32 or 226 of the Constitution. Once the election process commences with the notification of polls.

title: 'Lethal actions against commercial shipping not justified': EAM lodges 'strong protest' with Rubio
content: Jaishankar's remarks came in the aftermath of a US military strike on the commercial tanker MT Settebello in the Gulf of Oman, which claimed the lives of three Indian crew members. The vessel was targeted on Wednesday after US forces alleged that it had breached the naval blockade imposed on Iranian ports. Of the 24 Indian nationals aboard the tanker, 21 were rescued, while three were later confirmed dead.

title: ‘Phir Se’ recording session: Shashwat says Arijit was moved to tears; song felt divine to him
content: Composer Shashwat Sachdev revealed that Arijit Singh got emotional after recording Phir Se for Dhurandhar: The Revenge. Shashwat described it as a deeply special moment, saying Arijit's greatest contribution was the truth with which he entered the song, adding that their conversation afterwards will stay with him forever.

title: Sooryavanshi's viral look returns as Mudau's VAR reaction trends at FIFA WC
content: Football fans at the Mexico City stadium were amused by South African players' bewildered expressions during a FIFA World Cup match. A VAR announcement left them confused, reminding Indian fans of a similar viral moment involving cricketer Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. The incident occurred during the Mexico vs South Africa clash, highlighting a shared moment of bewilderment across sports.

title: Despite rift, Rahul Gandhi says DMK with opposition on ‘idea of India’
content: Alleging BJP steals elections, Rahul said he had warned “friends in TMC”, who were confident that they were sweeping Bengal polls, that they were in “dreamland” — stating he had seen it in Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Haryana and Maharashtra. He said BJP can’t win the next polls, “which is already won”, because people are angry, but the hurdle is that elections will not be free and fair.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's 10-year-old younger brother Aashirwad slams century
content: Younger brother Aashirwad Sooryavanshi, aged 10, has scored a brilliant century in a local practice match, mirroring the cricketing talent of his elder brother Vaibhav. Vaibhav, a teenage sensation, proudly shared Aashirwad's achievement on social media while he is currently on tour with India A.

title: Candidates get 15 minutes extra time for NEET retest
content: The retest comes after cancellation of the May 3 exam following paper leak, which is being probed by CBI. In a notice, NTA said the exam — for admissions to medical colleges — will be held from 2 pm to 5.15 pm. The additional 15 minutes will accommodate mandatory formalities (attendance signing and other procedures).

title: NDA looks to boost numbers in Lok Sabha, Sena UBT may also split
content: Sources within NDA suggest one of the parties that may see desertion from its ranks is Sena UBT, a possibility being talked about since the alliance swept the assembly polls in Maharashtra but has gained traction now. The Uddhav Thackeray-led party has nine MPs in Lok Sabha and six of them will have to merge with another party — Shiv Sena led by deputy CM Eknath Shinde seen as the most probable option — to avoid losing membership under the anti-defection law.

title: Rebels claim 19 MPs in ‘real TMC’ fight, set to meet Speaker
content: While none from the rebel camp contested the screenshots, almost everyone voiced grievance against Abhishek Banerjee. Lashing out, Abhishek said, “BJP has been trying many tactics — from creating rift in our party to intimidating party workers, taking over party offices, and destroying our organisation. But no matter what they do, they cannot deny the fact that 2.6 crore people voted for us and we got a 41% votes. We will continue to fight against this oppression.”

title: 'Peddi' BO day 9: Ram Charan film earns Rs 286.38 cr
content: Ram Charan's 'Peddi' continues its strong box office run, nearing the Rs 200 crore net mark in India despite a slight dip on Day 9. The sports action drama has amassed Rs 198.70 crore net domestically and Rs 286.38 crore worldwide. Critics praise Charan's committed performance, highlighting his portrayal of a fight for identity and self-respect.

title: From Kerala to latex: Tracing London-based designer Harri KS’s unlikely rise
content: Raised around rubber plantations in Kerala, designer Harri KS is rethinking latex, form, and the idea of wearability

title: Police search Abhishek Banerjee's Kolkata residence for over four hours
content: The operation, led by a team from Paschim Medinipur's Salboni Police station and assisted by Kolkata Police personnel, began shortly after 3 a.m. and continued for more than four hours, triggering sharp reactions from the TMC

title: ‘Deep-seated’ bias for male child persists despite improving sex ratio, says Supreme Court
content: The top court refused to quash criminal proceedings against a Maharashtra doctor under the Pre-Conception and Pre-Natal Diagnostic Techniques (Prohibition of Sex Selection) Act, 1994

title: No new Nipah cases in Kozhikode; three hospitalised for observation
content: Ten more people added to the updated contact list, taking the total number of existing contacts under surveillance to 87 on Friday. Of them, four were categorised as highest-risk contacts, 16 as high-risk and 67 as low-risk contacts.

title: The human cost of a deadly weed killer: banning of  paraqua in Telangana
content: In March, Telangana banned paraquat, one of the world’s most widely used and highly toxic weedicides, becoming only the third State in India to do so. The herbicide is responsible for hundreds of deaths each year, most of them by taking their own lives, and some accidental. Serish Nanisetti reports on the campaign that led to the ban and why much more remains to be done

title: Centre denies reports of fourth vessel attacked near Oman; says all crew members safe
content: MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal denied reports that MT Liaki Freedom was attacked on Friday night; he said the Ministry contacted the vessel and confirmed that all crew were safe

title: Flight headed to Kozhikode makes emergency landing at Kochi airport
content: As per established safety protocols, a local standby was declared, followed by a full emergency at 3.34 a.m. to ensure the readiness of all airport emergency response services

title: Monsoon 2026 LIVE: IMD issues red alert for thunderstorms, lightning in Delhi
content: Heavy rains that lashed several parts of Hyderabad on June 12 evening and continued through the night disrupted flight operations at the Rajiv Gandhi International Airport

title: Congress launches nationwide campaign on paper leaks, youth unemployment
content: Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi will be addressing student convention on June 17

title: NLC emerges as preferred bidder for a critical mineral block in Telangana
content: The announcement comes close on the heels of NLC signing an MoU with CSIR-Central Electrochemical Research Institute (CSIR-CECRI), Karaikudi, to foster collaboration in the field of critical and strategic minerals beneficiation and extraction technologies

title: Telangana sees tepid monsoon onset, no strong systems in sight
content: While there may be occasional bursts of heavy rain, most rainfall in the coming days will likely be light to moderate, according to meteorologists at the Telangana Development Planning Society

title: BJP behaving like North Korean leader Kim Jong Un: Revanth Reddy
content: After illegally snatching Meenakshi Natarajan’s seat, the BJP and BRS were indulging in a misinformation campaign  like allegations of ‘coverts’ within the Congress to only divert attention

title: Minimum one Sanskrit batch required for Classes 6 and 9 in Kendriya Vidyalayas
content: Keeping Sanskrit as an option in all KVs ensures a smooth transition for students facing frequent transfers, letting others choose regional languages; some KVs face challenges of limited teachers and resources

title: Jaishankar speaks to Rubio, lodges strong protest over U.S. Navy attacks that killed three Indians
content: “Spoke to U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio this evening. I reiterated India’s strong protest at the attacks by the U.S. Navy in the Gulf that killed three Indian mariners,” Mr. Jaishankar said.

title: AI-171 crash: AAIB releases interim statement on probe
content: Final report to be released after all investigative work has been completed, and the mandatory international review and consultation processes concluded, says Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau

title: Congress does not require neutrality of the Indian State to function: Rahul Gandhi
content: In a speech that outlined the road ahead for the INDIA bloc, the Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha asserted Congress’s primacy, acknowledged constraints, and urged unity amid sharp internal differences

title: Regional parties should rejoin Congress, strengthen Opposition unity: Ashok Gehlot
content: The former Chief Minister said safeguarding democratic values should be the foremost priority for all political forces, as the democracy in the country was “under serious threat”

title: Signature of rebels Trinamool MPs in public, many actor turned Parliamentarians switch sides
content: A majority of the rebel Trinamool Congress MPs are first time Parliamentarian, including cricketer turned politician Yusuf Pathan, actor Saayoni Ghosh and Cooch Behar MP Jagadish Verma Basunia among others

title: Murari Babu, former TDB official accused in Sabarimala gold theft case, passes away
content: Murari Babu has been ailing for some time, and the death occurred around 12.48 a.m., said authorities of the Amrita Hospital in Kochi

title: Supported by CJP, Lucknow students protest lack of transparency, irregularities in recruitment drives
content: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke joins protest; protesters demand independent high-level inquiry into Lekhpal exam irregularities, seek permanent grievance redressal mechanism for competitive exam aspirants

title: Punjab may go to polls in November, Mann to return as CM: Kejriwal
content: “I have been told that the elections will be held in November [2026], not February [2027]. Only four months are left. Now we all have only one task — to make Bhagwant Singh Mann Chief Minister again,” said the ruling party’s national convener.

title: Maharashtra to table ‘Women Farmers Empowerment Bill’ during upcoming Monsoon Session
content: Mr. Fadnavis reviewed a preliminary presentation on the draft Bill at a meeting attended by Deputy Chief Minister Sunetra Ajit Pawar, Agriculture Minister Dattatray Bharane and Minister of State for Agriculture Ashish Jaiswal at his official residence on Friday

title: Odisha CM announces free education from KG to PG in govt. institutions
content: The State launches Chief Minister Annapurna Yojana, under which all beneficiaries of the National Food Security Act and State Food Security Scheme will receive, an additional 5 kg of rice per month free of cost

title: No formal proposal yet, say senior NCP(SP) leaders amid buzz over merger with Congress
content: While the Congress’s Maharashtra unit has refused comment on speculations about the merger, sources said a proposal to this effect has been in the pipeline for some time.

title: ED arrests two former executives of Reliance Anil Ambani Group
content: The ED has taken transit remand of Sateesh Seth and Gautam Doshi, both of whom previously served as directors of Reliance Telecom Ltd., and is taking them to Delhi as the case is registered in the national capital, they said.

title: Will bring Tatas to Bengal but land at Singur no longer belongs to govt., says Bengal CM
content: The Chief Minister who has been in office for about five weeks says that benefits of the double-engine government are slowly reaching the people of West Bengal

title: Road, drainage works progressing along Palarivattom corridor
content: The laying of interlocking tiles has been completed over a short stretch in front of St. Martin’s Church at Palarivattom, while groundwork, including the spreading of metal and surface levelling, is under way

title: Invisible tropical waves linked to extreme rainfall, says study
content: The study reveals that invisible atmospheric disturbances known as Kelvin, Rossby and Mixed Rossby-Gravity waves significantly enhance heavy rainfall by organising deep convective cloud systems and increasing moisture convergence over the Arabian Sea and the Western Ghats

title: Police propose measures to tackle Kochi city’s traffic woes on a war footing
content: Identified traffic choke points include Edappally, Palarivattom, Cheranalloor, Edachira, Vyttila, Thiruvankulam, Thevara, Bolgatty Junction, Pallimukku, High Court Jn., Kaloor and Kadavanthra

title: High Court directs panel to proceed with demolition of Chander Kunj Army towers
content: Firm that demolished Maradu flats gets nod for twin towers’ demolition; court declines to stay award of demolition contract to Edifice Engineering despite challenge by lowest bidder

title: Malayidamthuruth land dispute talks hit hurdle
content: Minister-led meeting scheduled to discuss a proposal to avoid the eviction of seven Dalit families from the 2.69-acre Pariyathukavu settlement postponed to a later date

title: Power demand in Chennai hits a new high of over 5,000 MW
content: The demand was recorded on June 10, even as residents complain of power outages; previously, the maximum demand was 4,966 MW

title: One year since AI-171 crash, pilots body questions delay in releasing final probe report
content: The Federation of Indian Pilots urges authorities not to publish an incomplete interim report, warning that it could add to speculation on the cause of the June 2025 crash. Officials say engine manufacturer General Electric is still analysing data from AI-171 engines, contributing to the delay.

title: Amit Shah reviews Amarnath Yatra security
content: He directed the officials to ensure robust security arrangements at other major tourist destinations besides the Yatra route; this year’s Yatra is scheduled from July 3 to August 28

title: Defections, dissent and survival: What’s driving the crisis inside TMC
content: The Trinamool Congress is facing a major political crisis following defections, internal dissent, and questions over leadership. What’s driving the unrest inside TMC? Is this a temporary phase or a deeper shift in West Bengal politics? We break down the key developments, power struggles, and what lies ahead for Mamata Banerjee and her party.

title: Govt. non-committal on LDF’s free UG promise; FYUGP review on the cards
content: ₹1,000-a-month ‘Indira Guarantee’ for college-going girls will be implemented in the upcoming academic year, says Higher Education Minister Roji M. John

title: Stricter enforcement measures planned for CRZ violations, industrial pollution: T.N. Environment Minister
content: He says the government will step up monitoring on biomedical waste management, and a scientific assessment of solid waste generation will be taken up across selected areas to identify waste hotspots and infrastructure gaps

title: ITI management feud turns deadly in Bapatla district
content: Long-running dispute over the administration of an institute at Epurupalem village leaves one person dead and another critically injured

title: AIADMK plans to hold extensive, district-wise consultations shortly
content: Such an exercise will be helpful for the party, which has to face not only byelections to five Assembly constituencies but also local bodies’ polls in eight months, says a researcher of the party

title: Gold theft on Guruvayur-Chennai train: eight-member inter-state gang arrested
content: Railway Protection Force, in coordination with Tamil Nadu Government Railway Police, recover stolen property worth ₹2.5 crore

title: BRICS nations adopt resolution on people-centric urban development
content: The Ministerial declaration adopted on the concluding day of the two-day 13th BRICS Urbanisation Forum highlights key priorities shared by member nations and reaffirms commitment to advancing people-centred urban development

title: Attacks on ships ‘unacceptable’, says India; Trump blames Iran
content: Three ships attacked by U.S. had strong Indian links going beyond crew onboard; External Affairs Ministry summons Charge d’Affaires of U.S. Embassy Jason Meeks and lodges ‘strong protest’ for second time

title: One year on, families of Air India crash victims demand answers, accountability
content: Family members and the Federation of Indian Pilots bemoaned that there was no statement from the Prime Minister or the Gujarat Chief Minister, nor any public representative was present at the gathering

title: Anthropic Suspends Fable 5, Mythos 5 AI Models After US Security Order
content: Just three days after publicly launching Fable 5, the company said in a blog post that it received a government directive banning all foreign nationals from accessing Fable 5 and Mythos 5

title: Peace Deal Soon? US Official 80-85% Confident, But Iran Has Some Conditions
content: Though pitched as a major diplomatic victory, the proposed agreement will only establish a 60-day ceasefire that would be followed by another set of negotiations.

title: Madhur Virli's Rape 'Joke' Sparks Outrage, Adds To 'Rs 370 Ki Biryani' Row
content: A video from stand-up comedian Madhur Virli's 2024 show 'Love & Latex' has surfaced amid 'Rs 370 ki biryani' row and shows him making remarks about rape.

title: 3 Indians Died In US Strikes. 10 Others Were Killed When A Jet Fell From Sky
content: Three Indians were killed in an US strike on a vessel off the coast of Oman. It is not the first time that Indians have come under the line of fire from American miscalculations.

title: From Concerts To Bhajan Clubbing: Inside India's Rs 13,000-Crore Boom
content: According to a BookMyShow-EY Parthenon report, India's organised live entertainment market is already worth more than Rs 13,000 crore.

title: Glowing Orbs, Potato-like Object: In US' New UFO Files, No Answers On Aliens
content: A person whose name is blacked out reported coming home and seeing an intense light hovering below the trees in their backyard.

title: Leader Of "Bloodthirsty" Terror Outfit Tren De Aragua Killed In US Strike
content: Tren de Aragua has been labeled by the United States as a terrorist organization. Guerrero Flores was chargedwith racketeering conspiracy and other crimes, including lending support to terrorists in crimes

title: From Homes To Fuel: What Elon Musk's $1 Trillion Can Actually Buy
content: Musk's new title arrives amid a wider acceleration for the richest of the rich. Year after year, his former (although now very distant) billionaires club has reaped a growing number of members - from tech titans to celebrities.

title: 1 Dead, 10 Injured In Texas Shooting, Suspect Fired At Cop Days Earlier
content: The suspect, 45-year-old Victor Mata Villarreal, already was being sought by authorities when he began firing at police and bystanders in Midland on Friday

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Delhi Records Coolest June Day So Far As Rain Brings Relief From Heat
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Weather conditions are expected to improve after the next two days.

title: Trump Claims Iran Tried To Attack Indian Ships With Drones, Slams Deal 'Leak'
content: US President Donald Trump's comment comes after three vessels with Indian crew came under attack off the Omani coast this week.

title: India, Nepal Hold Talks On Joint Working Group On Cross-Border Railway Links
content: Both sides on Friday discussed the ongoing implementation of the Jaynagar-Bijalpura-Bardibas and Jogbani-Biratnagar broad-gauge railway lines between India and Nepal, being developed with grant assistance from the Government of India.

title: US-Iran War Live: Iran, US Say Deal Close, But Disagreements Persist Over Terms
content: US-Israel-Iran War News Live Updates: Trump said key points of a potential agreement have been worked out with support from several countries, raising hopes of a deal soon.

title: "Not Justified": S Jaishankar To Marco Rubio On Killing Of 3 Indian Sailors
content: The EAM S Jaishankar while speaking to his US counterpart Rubio on Friday evening also added that "such lethal actions against commercial shipping are 'not justified'".

title: Indian-Origin Man Jailed For 34 Years In UK For Kidnapping And Rape
content: The court heard that the woman became suspicious when she was asked to deliver a suitcase containing unknown items from Thailand back to the UK in June 2024.

title: US Court Rejects Bid To Halt Removal Of Trump Name From Kennedy Center
content: US federal judge on Friday rejected a bid by the board of the Kennedy Center and the Justice Department to halt the removal of President Donald Trump's name from the performing arts venue.

title: As Trump Predicts Iran War's End, Some Objectives Unfulfilled

content: Bharti Jain is senior editor with The Times of India, New Delhi. She has been writing on security matters since 1996. Having covered the Union home ministry, security agencies, Election Commission and the ‘prime’ political beat, the Congress, for The Economic Times all these years, she moved to TOI in August 2012. Her repertoire of news stories delves into the whole gamut of issues related to terrorism and internal strife, besides probing strategic affairs in India’s neighbourhood. Read More
